question: This area chart shows average housing prices ($1000s) across different cities. What makes this visualization misleading?

options.A: It suggests a generally smooth progression across the cities, as if they formed a continuous ordered scale.
options.B: Denver has by far the highest average housing price among all the cities shown.
options.C: Boston has by far the lowest average housing price among all the cities shown.
options.D: It inappropriately uses a single filled area across unrelated city categories, creating a false impression of a smooth trend between them.

explanation: This area chart joins category values for city into a continuous filled region. However, the x‑axis categories (e.g., Miami, Boston, Atlanta, Nashville, ...) have no inherent numeric or spatial continuity, so the apparent smooth trend is an artifact of the chart type rather than a meaningful pattern. A bar chart would allow readers to compare each category independently without implying a continuous progression.
